title: 以下、スタックしているので解決せよ。/kernelで進めて。 設計の主線を切り替えたので、回帰も切り替えます。strict readiness は standalone probe ではな く real bootstrap-backed kernel smoke を見る形にしました。 • Ran bash /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/tests/test-check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh └ PASS [required mode runs vote smoke] PASS [required mode runs k...
summary_text: **Findings (sidecar lane)** 1. **Most likely stall root cause: readiness timeout budget is still below bootstrap worst-case.** `check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh` wraps `/kernel` smoke in a global timeout (`SMOKE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` default `60`, strict floor `180`) at [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh:8`](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh#L8), [`:208`](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh#L208), [`:236`](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh#L236). `codex-prompt-launch` strict bootstrap can legitimately run much longer: Claude lane defaults to progressive `60/90/120s` across 3 attempts plus backoff (`2s`) at [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch:500`](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L500), [`:502`](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L502), [`:599`](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L599), [`:600`](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L600). The existing formula elsewhere already models this and yields ~`299s` default at [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh:28`](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh#L28). **Concrete hardening:** compute readiness kernel timeout from the same formula (or reuse a shared helper/env), not a fixed `180`. 2. **Variance amplifier: cache fast-path applies only to prompt `k`, but readiness executes prompt `kernel`.** `kernel` command always launches `codex-prompt-launch kernel` at [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/kernel:4`](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/kernel#L4). Cached bootstrap reuse in smoke mode is gated by `prompt_name == "k"` only at [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch:561`](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L561). So readiness’s strict `/kernel` smoke does cold bootstrap every run, increasing timeout risk. **Concrete hardening:** allow the same cache shortcut for `kernel` (not only `k`) or run readiness through `k` alias semantics. 3. **Secondary inconsistency (not direct stall cause): `run_provider_probes_if_needed` is defined but never called.** Function exists at [`/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh:144`](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh#L144), but script flow only calls `run_live_smoke` at [`:282`](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh#L282). `PROVIDER_PROBE_MODE=required` currently acts as a strict smoke behavior switch, not an actual standalone probe execution. **Concrete hardening:** either invoke probe function explicitly or rename mode/comments to reflect bootstrap-backed-only behavior and avoid operator confusion.
